headline: TSMC June revenue NT$442.7B +67.9% YoY, +6.2% MoM — all-time monthly high; Q2 $39.6B beats top of guidance
summary: TSMC reported June 2026 revenue of NT$442.68 billion, up 67.9% YoY and 6.2% MoM — an all-time monthly record. First-half revenue reached NT$2,404.48B (+35.6% YoY). Q2 revenue tallies to NT$1.27T (~$39.6B USD), above the top end of the company's own $39.0-40.2B guidance. Report was pushed from Jul 10 to Jul 13 by the Typhoon Bavi day-off. Sets a strong tape into the Jul 16 earnings call and offsets Asia peak-earnings anxiety.

headline: KOSPI triggers 7th circuit breaker of 2026: SK Hynix -15.4%, Samsung -10.7%, Kioxia -12.9% on peak-earnings + Middle East
summary: South Korea's KOSPI fell 8.07% to 6,806.94 on Monday, tripping a market-wide 20-min halt at 13:28 KST — the 7th circuit breaker of 2026. SK Hynix plunged 15.37% to KRW 1,845,000 (near one-month low); Samsung Electronics dropped 10.7% to KRW 254,500 (two-month low); Nikkei -1.92% with Kioxia -12.86%. Triggers: (i) Korea Investment & Securities cut SK Hynix Q2 op-profit forecast to KRW 60.4T vs KRW 65T Street (-8%), reviving peak-earnings fears; (ii) US-Iran Strait of Hormuz escalation drove risk-off + KRW weakness; (iii) profit-taking versus SK Hynix ADR premium (KS shares discounted vs SKHY $168.01 Fri close = +12.8% over $149 IPO). Foreign+institutional net-sold KRW 2.8T.

headline: US-Iran Strait of Hormuz clash: CENTCOM strikes + Iran missile/drone attacks on Gulf allies; Brent +4% to $78.82
summary: CENTCOM confirmed Sunday dozens of strikes on Iranian assets to degrade attacks on shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, triggered by the reported attack on the MV GFS Galaxy container ship. Iran launched a wave of missile and drone attacks on the UAE, Qatar, Kuwait, Oman and Bahrain in response. Brent Sep futures rose 4%+ to $78.82/bbl, the highest since Jun 22. Hormuz transits collapsed to 6-9 vessels from ~130/day pre-war, per Windward. Adds fresh macro overhang into today's US session and next-week bank earnings; direct read-across to LNG/gas exposure (Cheniere, GEV gas turbines), Middle-East hyperscaler builds, and defense spend.

headline: Meta +3-6% pre-market on SemiAnalysis 'Meta Compute' report refuting capex slowdown; 5GW contracted in H1, 2027 accelerates
summary: SemiAnalysis published a bullish research note stating Meta's datacenter and compute procurement is not slowing but accelerating, with 2027 capex forecast to be 'astonishingly high.' Report tallies over 5GW of datacenter+hosting capacity contracted in H1 2026 alone. Four validated demand pillars: Meta Superintelligence Labs, 10x scaling of ads recommendation, a ~$10B Anthropic Claude private-instance deal, and SpaceX-style external hosting arrangements. Meta shares +3-6% pre-market Monday; refutes the neocloud-fear narrative that dented META, CoreWeave and Nebius on Jul 1-3.

headline: US futures lower into busy earnings week: JPM/WFC/C Tue, ASML/GS/MS/BAC Wed, TSM/NFLX Thu; June CPI + Warsh testimony same day
summary: S&P 500 futures edged lower Monday as investors digest Middle East escalation and prepare for a heavy calendar: JPMorgan, Wells Fargo, Citigroup, BlackRock report Tuesday pre-open; Goldman, Morgan Stanley, Bank of America, ASML, UnitedHealth, J&J report Wednesday; TSMC, Netflix report Thursday. Consensus is Q2 S&P 500 EPS +24% YoY. June CPI drops Tuesday same-day as Fed Chair Warsh's House Financial Services testimony (Wed at Senate Banking) — headline projected at 3.8% YoY (from 4.2%), core 2.8%. Warsh has framed 2% inflation as the defining priority, sharpening rate-cut vs peak-earnings tension.

headline: Goldman Sachs raises Applied Materials PT to $645 from $520 on DRAM strength + order visibility into 2028
summary: Goldman Sachs' James Schneider maintained Buy on Applied Materials and lifted the 12-month price target to $645 from $520 (Jul 11). Rationale: DRAM WFE strength, pricing gains, and order visibility now stretching into 2028. Valuation basis: ~32x normalized ~$20 EPS. Follows AMAT's record fiscal Q2 revenue of $7.91B (+20% YoY) and EPS $2.86 vs $2.68 Street. Read-across to LRCX and KLAC WFE peers; supports the broader semi-cap re-rate that also lifted Bernstein's ASML PT to EUR 2,623 (Jul 7).

headline: ASML Q2 earnings preview (Wed Jul 15): guide EUR 8.4-9.0B, focus on net bookings, China and High-NA EUV commentary
summary: ASML reports Q2 2026 Wednesday Jul 15. Company guided net sales EUR 8.4-9.0B (mid EUR 8.7B) at 51-52% GM. April full-year guide raised to EUR 36-40B (from EUR 34-39B). Three watch items: net bookings (last disclosed Q4'25 = EUR 13.2B, of which EUR 7.4B EUV), China guidance under intensifying export-control pressure (Netherlands joined US-led Pax Silica alliance Jun 23), and High-NA EUV cadence. Zacks consensus EPS $7.98 (+75% YoY). Print is the primary global-fab bellwether alongside TSMC's Jul 16 call.

headline: Samsung analyst dispersion widens: DB Fin sets PT KRW 360k (below recent peak), KB Securities KRW 600k — 1.7x spread
summary: Korean sell-side dispersion on Samsung Electronics has widened sharply into today's -10.7% print: DB Financial Investment set its target at KRW 360,000 (below the recent peak stock price), while KB Securities offered KRW 600,000. The 1.7x gap and July's near-parity between upgrade (167) and downgrade (157) counts crystallize the peak-earnings debate as SK Hynix's ADR premium exposes DRAM/HBM cyclicality assumptions.

headline: SambaNova Series F closes $1B at $11B post-money; JPMorgan names it inference-infrastructure partner
summary: SambaNova Systems announced first close of a $1B Series F at $11B post-money on Jul 8, led by General Atlantic with BlackRock, T. Rowe, Capital Group, Battery, Intel Capital, Qatar Investment Authority, Vista Equity. Landmark customer: JPMorgan Chase selected SN40L/SN50 systems for secure on-premises AI inference. CEO Rodrigo Liang cited a 12-month supply-chain lock-in as use-of-proceeds. Competitive: inference-tier alternative to Nvidia at the enterprise/regulated tier — read-across to CRWV, NBIS and the neocloud ecosystem plus JPM disclosure at Tuesday's earnings call.
